findings.issue: ProfileProvider clears the stale access token on 401 only by grepping err.message for the string '401'. This is fragile: if the ApiError wording changes or a non-ApiError reaches the catch, the stale token is kept and the user will keep hitting the same auth failure.
findings.remediation: Import ApiError from lib/api/utils and check err instanceof ApiError && err.statusCode === 401.

findings.issue: InvitationCompletePage's 'verifier missing' error branch clears the invitation token and access token but omits clearVerifier(), while the 'token missing' branch clears the verifier and access token but omits clearInvitationToken(). Both branches therefore violate the 'clear all session keys on every error path' invariant for falsy-but-present storage values.
findings.remediation: Call both clearVerifier() and clearInvitationToken() in every error branch of InvitationCompletePage, like the catch block already does.

findings.issue: OAuthCallbackPage computes safeReturnTo but then discards it on every error redirect (oauthError, missing params, missing verifier, redeem failure). A user who started from a deep link and hits an OAuth/denied state is sent to /login without return_to, losing their original destination.
findings.remediation: Append &return_to=${encodeURIComponent(safeReturnTo)} to each /login?error=... redirect in OAuthCallbackPage.

findings.issue: generateVerifier() calls globalThis.crypto.getRandomValues unconditionally. On environments where window.crypto is undefined or lacks getRandomValues, sign-in fails before the SHA-256 fallback is even invoked; the fallback only covers hashing, not verifier entropy.
findings.remediation: Add a guard for globalThis.crypto?.getRandomValues; throw a descriptive error or fall back to a CSPRNG shim when the browser crypto API is absent.
